title: Fix admin login flow: Go to Login button broken and full auth flow non-functional

requirements.summary: Rewrite AdminGuard component with minimal implementation that directly triggers Internet Identity login popup inline without navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting /admin while unauthenticated shows 'Authentication Required' with a 'Go to Login' button
Clicking 'Go to Login' opens the Internet Identity authentication popup immediately without navigating away
After successful Internet Identity login, the admin-status query is immediately refetched
If no admin has been registered yet, a 'Claim Admin Access' button appears after login
Clicking 'Claim Admin Access' calls the backend claimAdmin function and redirects to /admin on success
If an admin is already registered and the logged-in user is not that admin, 'Access Denied' is shown with no claim button
If the logged-in user is the admin, the admin panel content renders correctly
The fix works correctly in both Safari and Chrome

file_operations.description: Rewrite the AdminGuard component from scratch with a completely new, minimal implementation. When unauthenticated, show 'Authentication Required' message and a 'Go to Login' button that directly calls login() from useInternetIdentity (no TanStack Router navigate). After login completes, immediately invalidate and refetch the admin-status query using queryClient. If authenticated and no admin is registered, show a 'Claim Admin Access' button that calls the backend claimAdmin function, handles all return variants (#success, #alreadyClaimed, #notAuthenticated), and on success redirects to /admin. If authenticated and another admin is registered, show 'Access Denied'. If authenticated and current user IS the admin, render children. Use useQueryClient for cache invalidation. Handle all loading and error states with appropriate UI feedback.

requirements.summary: Fix useActor hook to re-create actor with fresh authenticated identity immediately after login, eliminating race conditions
acceptanceCriteria: After Internet Identity login, the actor is re-initialized with the authenticated identity before checkAdminStatus is called
No race condition exists between auth state change and actor instantiation
Admin-status query is invalidated and refetched immediately after login completes
Admin-status query is invalidated and refetched immediately after a successful claimAdmin call
The flow works correctly on both first page load and on subsequent navigations to /admin

file_operations.description: Modify the useCheckAdminStatus hook to ensure it properly waits for actor initialization and is not called with a stale or anonymous actor. Add explicit enabled conditions that check both actor availability and actorFetching status. Ensure the query key includes identity-dependent information to trigger refetch on identity change. Add query options that prevent premature execution before the actor is ready. Verify the hook properly handles the race condition where login completes but the actor hasn't been re-initialized yet.

After calling login() from useInternetIdentity in the 'Go to Login' button handler, add an await to ensure login completes, then explicitly call queryClient.invalidateQueries for the admin-status query key. Similarly, after a successful claimAdmin call, immediately invalidate the admin-status query. Ensure proper error handling wraps both the login call and the claimAdmin mutation to catch and display errors to the user. Add loading state management during login and claimAdmin operations.
